mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll expansion signals robust labor demand, raising corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 6-month rolling growth is negative but SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of overlapping windows, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and fails to decline over a quarter, persistent tight monetary policy compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5 and FEDFUNDS level unchanged or higher over past 3 months
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and stable/rising but SPX 60-day returns are positive in >50% of such episodes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and elevated unemployment create stagflationary conditions that erode real earnings and consumer spending, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and UNRATE > 4% but SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of monthly observations, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, which precedes a broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines >2% MoM but SPX 60-day returns are positive in >50% of occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales reflect strong consumer spending, the primary driver of US GDP, which lifts corporate revenues and stock prices over a 20-day forward window.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 12-month growth > 3% but SPX 20-day returns are negative in >50% of months, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an imminent contraction in manufacturing output, which reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive months but SPX 20-day returns are positive in >50% of such episodes, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend GDP growth combined with a very tight labor market (unemployment below 4%) indicates an overheating economy that invites monetary tightening, which reverses equity gains over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY > 2% and UNRATE < 4% but SPX 60-day returns are positive in >50% of quarters, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% represents a rapid deterioration in the labor market that spurs negative sentiment and immediate selling pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 4.5% but SPX 5-day returns are positive in >50% of weekly observations, reject.
